meta.description: Sync from akave repo tag v0.4.4 — removes PolicyFactory contract, updates Storage and AccessManager ABIs, extracts named error constants, and refactors SDK connection management

execution_order: CHANGE-1: Update Storage contract ABI — replace src/blockchain/storage.json entirely from the new Go binding
CHANGE-2: Update AccessManager contract ABI — replace src/blockchain/access_manager.json entirely from the new Go binding
CHANGE-3: Synchronise error dispatch table — verify selector coverage and IgnoreOffsetError equivalent
CHANGE-4: Remove PolicyFactory from IPC client configuration and deployment
CHANGE-5: Refactor SDK struct — remove legacy NodeAPIClient field; add connection-pool field; update Close
CHANGE-6: Refactor IPC struct — replace direct gRPC connection field with connection-pool field
TEST-1: Update integration test for contract lifecycle to reflect renamed policy-deployment helper
TEST-2: Port new Block test-utility helper function
TEST-3: Update CLI-level integration test error-message expectations

changes.title: Update Storage contract ABI
changes.description: The Storage contract ABI was recompiled for tag v0.4.4 with 23 additions and 2 deletions. Read the full ABI embedded in the Go binding at meta.source_commit and replace the target JSON ABI and any generated Rust bindings entirely. Do not apply a partial patch.

changes.title: Update AccessManager contract ABI
changes.description: The AccessManager contract ABI was recompiled for tag v0.4.4 with 1 addition and 1 deletion. Read the full ABI embedded in the Go binding at meta.source_commit and replace the target JSON ABI and any generated Rust bindings entirely.

changes.title: Synchronise error dispatch table
changes.description: All error entries in the Go dispatch table were promoted from anonymous inline errors to named exported package-level variables (e.g. ErrBucketAlreadyExists, ErrOffsetOutOfBounds). The set of error names and their 4-byte selectors did not change; only the structure changed. The IgnoreOffsetError function was updated to use errors.Is() identity comparison instead of a string equality check. The Rust equivalent ignore_offset_error in provider.rs already uses a proper enum-variant match, which aligns with the new Go behaviour. Read private/ipc/errors.go at meta.source_commit to confirm no selector values changed and that the Rust error list in contract_errors.rs is complete relative to the Go dispatch table.

changes.title: Remove PolicyFactory from IPC client
changes.description: The PolicyFactory contract was removed from the IPC client. The Config struct lost its PolicyFactoryContractAddress field; the Client struct lost PolicyFactory, ListPolicyAbi, and PolicyFactoryAbi fields; ContractsAddresses lost its PolicyFactory field; Dial no longer loads policy ABIs or instantiates PolicyFactory; DeployContracts no longer deploys PolicyFactory or ListPolicy through a factory. The public DeployListPolicy method was renamed to TestDeployListPolicy and simplified to deploy a ListPolicy directly without a factory. Read private/ipc/client.go at meta.source_commit for the authoritative new struct definitions. If the Rust SDK has any equivalent policy-factory configuration field or deployment path, remove it.

changes.title: Refactor SDK struct — remove NodeAPIClient, add connection pool
changes.description: The Go SDK struct removed the client field (a non-IPC NodeAPIClient) and added a pool field (a connection pool). The New constructor no longer creates a NodeAPIClient instance on startup. The Close method was updated to close the connection pool in addition to the gRPC connection. If the Rust AkaveSDK struct holds a similar non-IPC gRPC client that is now unused, remove it. If connection pooling exists in the Rust SDK, update teardown to close the pool as well. Read sdk/sdk.go at meta.source_commit for the authoritative new struct definition.

changes.title: Refactor IPC struct — replace direct connection with connection pool
changes.description: The Go IPC struct replaced its direct conn grpc.ClientConn field with a pool connectionPool field. The IPC() factory method now passes sdk.pool into the IPC struct instead of sdk.conn. Read sdk/sdk_ipc.go at meta.source_commit for the full new struct definition. Apply the equivalent change in the Rust AkaveSDK struct if a raw connection is held directly rather than through a pool abstraction.

contract_updates.notes: ABI recompiled for v0.4.4 with significant additions (+23 lines, -2 lines). Replace target ABI entirely.
ABI recompiled for v0.4.4 with minor change (+1 line, -1 line). Replace target ABI entirely.
Contract removed entirely from the SDK (all 408 lines deleted). Remove any target binding for this contract.

test_updates.description: Tests full bucket and file lifecycle including policy deployment. Updated to call the renamed TestDeployListPolicy (previously DeployListPolicy) which now directly deploys a ListPolicy contract without going through a PolicyFactory.
port_instructions: 1. Find the existing integration test for contract lifecycle in the Rust test suite.

2. Replace any call to a deploy_list_policy_via_factory helper with a direct deploy_list_policy call, mirroring the simplified Go implementation that calls DeployListPolicy and then Initialize directly.
3. Update variable names from policy_factory to list_policy where they hold the deployed ListPolicy instance.
4. Verify the test still calls assign_role and validate_access on the list-policy instance after deployment.
5. Ensure the DeployContracts call no longer passes a policy_factory_address (that config field is gone).

test_updates.description: New test-utility function that generates an IPFS block with random data of a specified size. Fills a buffer with random bytes, computes a SHA2-256 multihash, creates a CIDv1 with DagProtobuf codec, and wraps data and CID together.
port_instructions: 1. In the Rust test-utility module, add a block generation helper function.
2. Accept a size parameter (number of bytes).
3. Fill a Vec<u8> of that length with cryptographically random data using rand::thread_rng.
4. Hash the bytes with SHA2-256 using multihash::Code::Sha2_256.digest(&data).
5. Construct a CIDv1 with cid::DagProtobuf codec: Cid::new_v1(0x70, mh).
6. Return a tuple or struct containing the raw bytes and the CID.
7. Expose the function from the test utilities module.

test_updates.description: CLI integration test that verifies error messages returned to the user. The expected output strings changed from wrapped gRPC error messages to the raw contract error names: 'failed to get bucket: not found' became 'BucketNotFound', and 'file not exists' became 'BucketNotFound'. This reflects the improved error propagation from the updated errors.go that uses errors.Is() matching on named error variables.
port_instructions: 1. Locate the equivalent CLI-level integration test in the Rust test suite that checks error output when a bucket does not exist.
2. Update the expected error message string from the old wrapped message (e.g. 'not found' or similar) to 'BucketNotFound' to match the new contract error name.
3. Similarly update any test that expected 'file not exists' to instead expect 'BucketNotFound'.
4. Run the test against a local anvil node to confirm the new error message is surfaced correctly.
